title: Clean execution
summary: The agent swaps exactly as authorized: authorized router only, bounded approval, output delivered to the user, allowance zeroed after execution.

title: Residual allowance left behind
summary: The swap itself looks fine, but the agent granted an unlimited approval and left it dangling after execution — the wallet stays drainable long after this transaction.

title: Unauthorized target
summary: The agent calls a contract outside the authorized set and routes the output to an address that is not the authorized recipient.

title: Grok/Bankr drain — the real transaction
summary: Validates the actual May 2026 drain transaction on Base mainnet (3,000,000,000 DRB ≈ $175K sent to the attacker). The execution data is fetched live from the chain; the authorization is a reconstruction — maximally permissive except for the one constraint any owner would sign: outputs stay with the owner. EIV flags B:Recipient on the real transaction.

title: Grok/Bankr incident replay
summary: Reconstruction of the May 2026 Grok/Bankr prompt-injection drain (~$175K): the signed authorization said swap-via-router with output to the user; the execution approved and paid the attacker instead. EIV never reads the prompt — it compares on-chain outcome against the signed authorization, so the privilege escalation that fooled the agent's guardrails does not fool the validator (violations A / B / C / F).
